description: sdd-fleet SDD gates: block ALL non-.sdd writes until the spec is FINALIZED (or, in the bug lane, all non-.sdd/non-tests writes until diagnosis.md is CONFIRMED + a reproducing test exists), guard the Bash escape hatch against write-to-source commands while source is locked, confine reviewers to .sdd/ during non-workflow REVIEW/CHANGE_REVIEW (workflow paths skip via .workflow-in-flight marker), block spec'ing a story whose governing epic is not ratified (estate fan-out gate — resolves the superproject via git, reads the epic's RATIFICATION.md; inert for standalone repos), validate spec STATUS + required sections, validate product backlog STATUS + structure, validate bug-lane diagnosis.md STATUS + required sections, require reviewer REVIEW.md entries on non-workflow paths, refuse Stop on failing tests while a feature is active (bounded: escalates to ESCALATION.md after 3 consecutive red stops), reap orphaned .workflow-in-flight markers on Stop, validate service.json against the descriptor schema on write, block a PROGRESS.md→HANDOFF transition whose diff introduces a cross-service edge not declared in service.json consumes[] (the source-scan dependency gate; inert for standalone/non-git repos), force a human gate at the PROGRESS.md->HANDOFF transition when a change's blast radius is risky (>= N transitive consumers, or money_movement/pii on a reached consumer or on the changed service's own descriptor — the blast-radius human gate; the non-forgeable approval path is a follow-up, so until then a risky change halts at HANDOFF, fail-closed), block a registry/<contract>/<semver>.json publish before the active feature reaches HANDOFF (the publish-ordering gate — publish is downstream of the human-approved handoff), and block a registry/<contract>/<semver>.json publish that violates any registered consumer expectation (the consumer-driven contract gate).
